mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. As retail sales have been increasing steadily in the post-recession recovery, this trend signals continued economic expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales have grown over the past year, supporting consumer confidence
failure_condition: If the trailing 12-month growth rate of RSAFS is negative while S&P 500 still rises, or if the correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is negative over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.0% signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate profits and stock prices higher. The current rate is 6.3%, so a cross below 6.0% will activate this signal.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has been declining from its 2010 peak of 10.0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 falls in the 20 trading days after UNRATE drops below 6.0% in more than half of the occurrences, or if the signal has no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which increases corporate earnings and signals economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The series has been on a steady uptrend since 2009.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has increased year-over-year for 4 consecutive months as of April 2014
failure_condition: If the trailing 6-month growth rate of INDPRO is negative and S&P 500 still posts positive returns, or if the correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive over out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) is negative, monetary policy is accommodative, lowering discount rates and supporting equity valuations. The interaction between low nominal rates and moderate inflation produces a positive tailwind for stocks.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:At 0.09%, FEDFUNDS is well below the CPI inflation rate of about 2%
failure_condition: If the real rate becomes positive and the S&P 500 still produces positive returns, or if the forward returns under negative real rates are not significantly greater than under positive real rates (t-test p>0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (<=0.25%), the Fed's commitment to accommodative policy and potential quantitative easing supports risk assets. This regime, beginning in 2008, has produced generally positive equity returns as the economy recovers.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been at 0-0.25% since December 2008
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is at 0.25% or lower, or if the strategy has a negative Sharpe ratio over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 1,000 thousand units signals a robust housing market, which boosts consumer wealth through home construction and related spending, lifting GDP and stock prices over a longer horizon. Current HOUST at 946 is close to this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have been recovering from a trough of 478 in April 2009
failure_condition: If after HOUST exceeds 1000, the S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or below the unconditional mean more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's predictive accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both employment (PAYEMS rising) and unemployment (UNRATE falling) indicates a synchronized labor market strengthening. This joint behavior provides a more robust signal of consumer spending power and economic momentum, boosting short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 150K and UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls have been increasing by over 200K per month on average in 2014, while UNRATE has fallen from 6.7% to 6.3%
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal's Rank IC over a 2-year period is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A strong short-term growth spurt in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates accelerating manufacturing activity, which can lead to positive earnings surprises and boost equity prices in the near term. Specifically, when INDPRO's 3-month growth rate exceeds 1%, it signals a pickup in cyclical demand.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO grew from 101.8 in January 2014 to 102.74 in April 2014, a 3-month growth of about 0.9%, close to 1%
failure_condition: If after INDPRO 3-month growth exceeds 1%, the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal's cumulative return over out-of-sample periods is negative.
